description: Runtime lookup table mapping release-channel gate IDs to the GitHub Actions workflows/jobs/check-runs that satisfy them. The channel-resolver queries this via `gh api /repos/:o/:r/commits/:sha/check-runs` to decide how far up the channel ladder to promote. Authoritative for the resolver; the human-readable mapping lives in config/release/channels.json under `gates`.

gateEvaluation.comment: How recent a passing check-run must be to count toward gate satisfaction for the current SHA. 26h = 24h trigger interval + 2h buffer for clock skew / queue time. Older greens are ignored (they pre-date the trigger).
gateEvaluation.failModeComment: If a gate's workflow is unreachable / API errors / no runs found in the window, the resolver fails OPEN (treats as missing-signal, not blocking). Rationale: a transient CI infra outage should not block releases indefinitely. Stable promotion has its own manual human approval gate as the backstop.
gateEvaluation.conclusionRequiredComment: Only `success` (green) counts. `neutral` and `skipped` are treated as missing-signal. `failure`, `timed_out`, `cancelled`, `action_required`, `startup_failure`, `stale` are blocking.
gateEvaluation.excludePullRequestsComment: Check-runs from PRs are ignored — only pushes-to-main and workflow_dispatch runs count, so an in-flight feature branch can't accidentally satisfy a gate for a SHA on main.

build.comment: Build matrix + npm pack policy. Located in ci.yml. The resolver queries both names as separate check-runs.

unit.comment: Node 24 unit tests across 8 shards. The single aggregate check-run from ci.yml reports per-shard results in the summary.

integration.comment: Integration tests — 2 shards. The single check-run is enough (sharding is internal).

e2e.comment: Playwright E2E — 9 shards aggregated into one check-run.

release-green.comment: Full pre-flight validation: typecheck, lint, db-rules, docs, unit, vitest, integration, pack-artifact. Uses scripts/quality/validate-release-green.mjs.

resilience.comment: Three separate jobs in nightly-resilience.yml — each must be green.

mutation.comment: Stryker mutation testing — slow, advisory in most channels.

property.comment: fast-check property-based tests with random seed.

chaos.comment: Chaos engineering drill (pod kill + latency injection). Runs weekly.

fuzz.comment: cargo-fuzz build + list. Note: project has BOTH fuzz-ci.yml and fuzz-weekly.yml; they're identical files. We bind to fuzz-weekly.yml for the release gate (less frequent, more deliberate).

load.comment: k6 load test — three scenarios share the same `k6-run` job; the resolver treats any green k6 check-run in the window as satisfying `load`.

cross-platform.comment: CLI build + unit tests across ubuntu/macos/windows. The check-run names match the matrix entries.

a11y.comment: axe accessibility suite (Playwright) — freeze-and-alert strategy (does not block nightly).

channelCoverage.comment: Per-channel coverage matrix answering 'is the CI matrix lacking for E2E/integration and others such as chaos, mut, fuzz, perf, viz, reg?'. Built from the existing nightly + weekly workflows. Each cell is one of: required | advisory | n/a. Built automatically from channels.json but denormalized here for the resolver + dashboard.

integration.comment: WAS missing from PR→release fast-gate before validate-release-green.mjs (see comment in that script about v3.8.42 release PR).

release-green.comment: Pre-flight script in scripts/quality/validate-release-green.mjs. Runs on the nightly-release-green.yml schedule + manual dispatch.

mutation.comment: Stryker, slow → stays advisory until rc.

chaos.comment: Only in chaos-weekly.yml. NOT nightly — runs Mondays 02:00 UTC.

fuzz.comment: fuzz-ci.yml runs daily 02:00 Sat; fuzz-weekly.yml is identical. The release gate binds to fuzz-weekly for predictability.

load.comment: k6-load-test.yml runs daily at 04:00 UTC, scenario=smoke. Heavier scenarios are manual dispatch.

cross-platform.comment: cross-platform.yml is workflow_dispatch-only — no schedule. Effective coverage: on-demand.

a11y.comment: Lives in nightly-resilience.yml as the `a11y` job. Freeze-and-alert strategy means failure does NOT block — but it IS captured here as a check-run the resolver can consult.

visualization.comment: ASCII view of which gate lives in which schedule band. Empty = not run. X = runs.

coverageGaps.comment: Gaps the matrix currently has relative to the channel taxonomy's required gates. These are the gates that exist as concepts but have no workflow scheduled to produce them on a cadence the resolver can rely on for an automated promotion.

gaps.rationale: cross-platform.yml is workflow_dispatch-only. To make stable fully automatable we'd need to schedule it (e.g. weekly). Until then, stable's `cross-platform` requirement is satisfied by manual run before promotion. Documented as a known limitation.

LTS branches don't have a nightly-release-green.yml attached to them. Backport CI re-runs unit/vitest/integration on the lts branch but not the full release-green pre-flight. Tracked as a TODO — needs a parametrized reusable workflow.
